cases.title: BCO 34-1 Petitions re: Central Indiana Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The SJC decided the BCO 34-1 petitions regarding Central Indiana Presbytery by reference to the April 5, 2023 final decision in Case 2022-10 (PCA v. Herron), having previously postponed consideration of these petitions until after the Herron trial concluded.

cases.title: BCO 34-1 Petitions re: Missouri Presbytery

parties.complainant_or_appellant: Petitioners (Southeast Alabama Presbytery, Grace Presbytery via Overtures 36 and 37)

cases.synopsis: Two overtures from Southeast Alabama and Grace Presbyteries sought GA assumption of original jurisdiction over TE Greg Johnson of Missouri Presbytery for alleged doctrinal errors; the SJC dismissed the petitions (22-1) because TE Johnson and Memorial Presbyterian Church withdrew from the PCA in December 2022, depriving the PCA of jurisdiction.

cases.title: Miller v. Hills & Plains Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The SJC found Mr. Miller's complaint administratively out of order (22-0) because he filed it with the Session six months after the Session's action (May 19, 2021), well beyond the 60-day deadline required by BCO 43-1.

cases.title: Oh v. Korean Southeast Presbytery
cases.synopsis: TE Sung Bok Oh withdrew his complaint against Korean Southeast Presbytery on August 29, 2022.

cases.title: Murdock v. South Florida Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The SJC found the complaint administratively out of order (23-0) because complainant RE Terry Murdock died on November 15, 2022, and no other party had joined the original complaint.

cases.title: Michelson et al. v. Northwest Georgia Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The SJC found the complaint out of order and moot (23-0) because the Session had dismissed all charges against the three Ruling Elders involved and those REs voluntarily resigned and had their calls dissolved on July 23, 2022, settling the underlying dispute.

cases.title: Benyola v. Central Florida Presbytery (three complaints)
cases.synopsis: The SJC found all three complaints by Mr. Peter Benyola against Central Florida Presbytery administratively out of order (23-0) because Benyola was no longer a member of any PCA congregation and thus lacked standing.

cases.title: Wilson et al. v. Pacific Northwest Presbytery

parties.complainant_or_appellant: Mr. Derek Wilson et al. (Concerned Members of Covenant Presbyterian Church, Issaquah, WA)

precedent_refs: Michelson and Michelson v. Northwest Georgia Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The SJC found the complaint judicially out of order (20-2) because BCO 43-1 permits complaints only against acts of a court of the church, not against actions taken in a congregational meeting; the case involved complainants who alleged minor communicant members were wrongly prevented from voting on accepting their associate pastor's resignation, with concurring and dissenting opinions extensively addressing the question of minor communicant suffrage rights and the scope of BCO 43 jurisdiction.
